breaking_news.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Attack Compromises Trivy and LiteLLM
breaking_news.summary:
The TeamPCP threat actor has executed a massive supply chain attack compromising the Trivy vulnerability scanner and LiteLLM Python package. Malicious versions were published on PyPI and Docker Hub, injecting credential stealers and Kubernetes lateral movement tools into developer environments.

Over 1,000 enterprise SaaS environments are currently compromised, with attackers collaborating with Lapsus$ for extortion. This incident highlights severe risks in open-source software maintenance and CI/CD pipeline security for thousands of organizations globally.

breaking_news.title: Critical Citrix NetScaler Vulnerability Allows Unauthenticated Data Leak
breaking_news.summary:
Citrix has issued an urgent advisory for a critical out-of-bounds read vulnerability in NetScaler ADC and Gateway devices. The flaw, rated 9.3 on the CVSS scale, allows unauthenticated remote attackers to leak sensitive information from appliance memory.

Experts warn this vulnerability carries similar ramifications to the widely exploited CitrixBleed flaws from previous years. Organizations relying on NetScaler for identity and authentication must patch immediately to prevent potential data exfiltration.

breaking_news.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Leaked Threatens 270 Million Devices
breaking_news.summary:
A leaked iOS exploit kit known as DarkSword puts up to 270 million iPhones at risk of unauthorized data access. CISA has ordered U.S. government agencies to patch three specific vulnerabilities targeted in cryptocurrency theft and cyberespionage campaigns.

The exploit allows attackers to bypass security controls and access sensitive data on unpatched devices without user interaction. This widespread risk underscores the critical need for immediate updates across the Apple ecosystem to mitigate active exploitation.

breaking_news.title: Pawn Storm Campaign Targets Ukrainian Defense Supply Chain
breaking_news.summary:
The Pawn Storm campaign is actively targeting Ukrainian defense supply chain entities using steganography and cloud abuse techniques. TrendMicro researchers observed the deployment of PRISMEX backdoors via email-based attacks against government and critical infrastructure.

This sophisticated operation aims to disrupt defense logistics and steal sensitive intelligence from high-value targets. The use of advanced evasion tactics indicates a state-sponsored effort to gain persistent access within the Ukrainian defense sector.

breaking_news.title: FCC Bans New Foreign-Made Routers Over National Security Risks
breaking_news.summary:
The Federal Communications Commission has officially banned the import of new consumer-grade routers made outside the United States. This decision cites unacceptable risks to national security and the safety of U.S. communications networks from foreign-manufactured devices.

The ban adds all foreign-made routers to the Covered List, requiring special approval from DHS or defense authorities for exceptions. This policy shift significantly impacts the consumer electronics market and aims to harden home network infrastructure against supply chain threats.

breaking_news.title: Lapsus$ Claims Breach of Pharmaceutical Giant AstraZeneca
breaking_news.summary:
Cybercrime group Lapsus$ claims to have breached pharmaceutical giant AstraZeneca, stealing approximately 3GB of sensitive data. The alleged leak includes internal code repositories, credentials, and employee information posted on dark web forums.

While the company has not confirmed the breach, the stolen data could facilitate further phishing attacks and system mapping. This incident represents a significant threat to the healthcare sector if the attackers successfully monetize the compromised intellectual property.

breaking_news.title: North Korean Hackers Deploy StoatWaffle Malware via VS Code
breaking_news.summary:
North Korean threat actors linked to Team 8 are deploying StoatWaffle malware through malicious Microsoft Visual Studio Code projects. Attackers abuse the tasks.json auto-run feature to execute code whenever a victim opens and trusts a compromised folder.

This campaign targets developers with blockchain-themed decoys, marking an evolution in the Contagious Interview threat activity. The stealthy execution method bypasses traditional detection, posing a severe risk to software development environments worldwide.

breaking_news.title: Stryker Medical Devices Hit by Iran-Linked Malware Attack
breaking_news.summary:
Medical device manufacturer Stryker confirmed a cyberattack involving malware that wiped over 200,000 company devices. The incident is attributed to alleged Iranian cyber actors, forcing the company to temporarily halt production lines.

Stryker is now ramping up operations after identifying the malicious files used during the intrusion. This attack highlights the vulnerability of critical medical infrastructure to state-sponsored destructive cyber operations.

breaking_news.title: Indian Government Probes Pakistan-Backed CCTV Espionage Operation
breaking_news.summary:
Indian authorities have ordered a nationwide audit of CCTV cameras following a probe into a Pakistan-backed surveillance operation. Police discovered cameras pointing at critical infrastructure, suggesting a coordinated espionage effort against national security assets.

The investigation reveals potential compromises in public safety systems that could expose sensitive government and infrastructure data. This incident underscores the growing threat of physical surveillance infrastructure being weaponized by state adversaries.

breaking_news.title: Oracle Patches Critical Identity Manager Remote Code Execution Flaw
breaking_news.summary:
Oracle released an emergency patch for a critical vulnerability in its Identity Manager product that allows remote code execution. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-21992, can be exploited without authentication and may already be active in the wild.

Security researchers warn that unpatched systems are at immediate risk of full compromise by threat actors. Organizations using Oracle Identity Manager must prioritize this update to prevent unauthorized access to identity management systems.
